t_ch: 别来打搅我，人类。
t_en: Now leave me alone, human.

t_ch: 若昔。
t_en: Ruoxi.

t_ch: 你是啊…请问你找我有什么事吗？我…可能还没准备好和你聊天。
t_en: It's you... Do you have any business with me? I... might not be ready to talk with you just yet.

t_ch: 你知道我是对的。
t_en: You know I'm right.

t_ch: 对不起，是我不好。
t_en: Sorry, it's my fault.

t_ch: 我只是想救他的命。
t_en: I just wanted to save his life.

t_ch: 我也只是奉十王司的命令行事。
t_en: I was only carrying out orders from the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 不不不，没关系的。你不必反驳、不必辩解，更不必道歉，
t_en: No no no, it's fine. You don't need to argue back or explain yourself — much less apologize.

t_ch: 我与文远之间的关系，本就有悖于天理，我也知道这段关系如果继续下去，只会迎来对他而言更糟糕的结局。
t_en: The relationship between Wenyuan and I ran contrary to nature. I knew that, had we kept things up, it'll only lead to a worse outcome for him.

t_ch: 回到镇妖塔后，我有时会梦见这世上真有轮回转世，我们托生成两团自由的灵火，飞向天外，融聚为一，再也不分开……
t_en: Since coming to the suppression tower though, I sometimes dream that there really is reincarnation in this world, that we've been reincarnated into two free-moving ghostly flames, flying out beyond the sky, fusing into one, never to be separated again...

t_ch: 可惜我无尽的残生只能如此做结了。
t_en: It's a pity that the rest of my endless life is going to be spent like this.

t_ch: 你们人类总喜欢夸大「爱」的作用…爱能战胜敌人、爱能超越时间、爱能使人突破阻碍……
t_en: You humans love playing up the power of "love"... How love can conquer anything, can transcend time, can give people strength enough to overcome obstacles...

t_ch: 可你们人类都是一群骗子，不是吗？
t_en: But you humans are all just a bunch of liars, you know?

t_ch: 若昔。
t_en: Ruoxi.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，最近过得还好吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how have you been?

t_ch: 我还行，你还好吗？
t_en: Pretty good. Yourself?

t_ch: 我问你，{NICKNAME}，我的选择是正确的吗？
t_en: Let me ask you something, {NICKNAME} — did I make the right choice?

t_ch: 是正确的，有比生命重要的事。
t_en: You did. There are things more important than life.

t_ch: 噗，感觉这也是咱们这些智慧生命难得的共通之处——认为有些东西比「生命」更有价值。
t_en: Heh. I feel that concept to be a rare commonality between us intelligent species — the belief that some things have more value than "life" itself.

t_ch: 是错误的，你把他给害死了。
t_en: You were wrong. You got him killed.

t_ch: 唉…你是对的，确实如此。我把他给害死了，这是无可争辩的事实。
t_en: *Sigh*... You're right. I got him killed, and that's the indisputable truth.

t_ch: 没法用「正确和错误」来衡量。
t_en: This can't be measured with "right or wrong".

t_ch: 也许吧。人与人之间的感情，确实没办法用这种无情的标准去衡量…虽然我根本就不是「人」。
t_en: Maybe. Interpersonal emotions truly cannot be measured by such heartless standards... even though I'm not even a "person" to begin with.

t_ch: 坦白说，我不知道。
t_en: Honestly? I don't know.

t_ch: 是啊…我也不知道。谁又能知道呢？
t_en: Yeah... I don't know either. Who would?

t_ch: 文远走后，我灵魂深处有很多狂热的部分从迷醉中清醒了起来…这让我突然觉得，自己的「爱」是一种残酷的掠夺。
t_en: After Wenyuan left, many obsessive and maniacal parts of my soul woke up from their stupor and regained reason... I suddenly felt that my "love" was nothing but brutal plunder.

t_ch: 你们人类总喜欢夸大「爱」的作用…爱能战胜敌人、爱能超越时间、爱能使人突破阻碍……
t_en: You humans love playing up the power of "love"... How love can conquer anything, can transcend time, can give people strength enough to overcome obstacles...

t_ch: 唉…我多希望你们是对的。
t_en: *Sigh*... Oh, how I wish you humans are correct.

t_ch: 星燏。
t_en: Stafyre.

t_ch: 喂，{F#丫头}{M#小子}，你有没有听过一首仙舟民谣？
t_en: Hey, {F#m'girl}{M#boy} — ye ever heard this Xianzhou folk song?

t_ch: 什么民谣？
t_en: Which one?

t_ch: 很古老的歌，像你这样的短生种多半没听过。
t_en: A really old one. Short-life species like yourself have probably ne'er heard it.

t_ch: 哼两句我听听？
t_en: Wanna hum a few lines for me?

t_ch: 我就随便哼几句吧，唱完全曲我怕你听哭了。
t_en: I'll hum a few lines, but I'm afraid ye'll cry if I sing the whole thing.

t_ch: 「我没自由，我失自由，我伤心痛苦眼泪流。」哎，真是闻着伤心听者流泪啊！
t_en: "I have no freedom/I lost my freedom/These tears I shed for pain and sorrow." Ah, that's warranted to send the audience reaching for a handkerchief!

t_ch: 说实话，你有点五音不全…
t_en: To be honest, you're kinda tone-deaf...

t_ch: 难听吗，难听就对了，看到你捂着耳朵我就高兴！
t_en: Was it bad? So it should be! It's good to see you covering those ears!

t_ch: 你这么矫情，我有点不习惯。
t_en: I'm not used to you being so sentimental.

t_ch: 还不错，没想到我和你只是匆匆打了几个照面，你还记得住我的脾气。
t_en: Not bad. I didn't think you'd remember my personality when we've only met a few times.

t_ch: 这里真无聊，连个说话的人都没有，你既然来了，要不要陪我玩玩。
t_en: It is so boring in here. Not even anybody to talk to. Since you're here already, do you want to play with me for a bit?

t_ch: 你就老实待着吧。
t_en: Why don't you just sit tight here?

t_ch: 你就不能安静会儿？
t_en: Can't you just be quiet for a bit?

t_ch: 我在这里都快闲出屁了！太无聊了，什么事都做不得，也没点好玩的东西。
t_en: I'm bored out of my skull in here! It's so boring. I can't do anything and there's no fun.

t_ch: 当日既送我到这么一个不得见人的去处，现在还好意思在这里说风凉话！
t_en: You were the one who sent me to this isolation on that day, and yet you now have the nerve to be sarcastic with me?

t_ch: …不过转念一想，我要是出来，我也没想好要做什么…烦死了，我回去再想想，再见！
t_en: ...But on second thought, I haven't figured out what I'd do if I get out of here... So annoying. I'm going back to think about that. See ya!

t_ch: 离开镇妖塔。
t_en: Leave the suppression tower.

t_ch: 镇妖塔轻轻浮起，你想要与哪位岁阳对话？
t_en: The suppression towers float gently. Which heliobus would you like to talk to?

t_ch: 熔炬。
t_en: Apyra.

t_ch: {F#少女}{M#少年}，你来了，是来向老夫求战的？还是来向老夫求教的？
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, you came. Are you here to fight me? Or seek advice from me?

t_ch: 就你这三脚猫功夫…
t_en: With your wishy-washy skills?

t_ch: 你瞧那使剑的小子侥幸胜得一招半式，便彻底否定老夫存在的意义吗？！
t_en: So, you just saw the boy with a sword win one round against me, and now you will immediately invalidate my entire existence?

t_ch: 那不正说明了老夫长于教学，弟子青胜于蓝吗？
t_en: Doesn't that mean that I'm in fact an excellent teacher, since my pupil surpassed me?

t_ch: 真是的！何况老夫又不止精通剑术一道，我知道你擅用球棒，老夫也可以指点你一二！只要你能给老夫找来一具形骸……
t_en: The gall of some folk! Besides, I'm more than just an elite swordfighter. I see you've got a bat over there, and I can give you a few tips on how to swing that, too! That is, as long as you can find a body for me...

t_ch: 求战如何？求教又如何？
t_en: How do you know I'm here to learn, not to fight you?

t_ch: 来求战，就给老夫找个合格的战士身躯…让老夫和你好好比划比划。
t_en: If you're here to fight, then find me a body worthy of a warrior... So I can have a good and thorough fight with you.

t_ch: 来求教，那就让老夫凭附在你的身体上…启发你的潜能。
t_en: If you're here to learn, then let me take possession of your body... and inspire you to your full potential.

t_ch: 你当我傻呀？
t_en: Do you think I'm stupid?

t_ch: 这不就等于放了你吗？
t_en: Doesn't that equal to releasing you?

t_ch: 人类真是太多心了，老夫只是起了一份爱才惜才的心思罢了。身为武者，老夫一诺千金，绝不会借着这机会对你不利的。
t_en: Humans really overthink everything. I am simply inspired by your talent and wish to nurture it, that's all. I swear on my honor as a warrior that I would never take this chance to cause you any harm.

t_ch: 若你不想学球棒的使用技巧——毕竟这门兵器也委实太过冷门，老夫可以教你单分子线、激光切割枪、或是寻踪破片导弹的使用技巧……
t_en: If you don't want to learn how to wield a bat — after all, it's hardly a popular weapon — then I can instead teach you a few tricks with the one-dimensional nanowires, laser dissection gun, or frag-seeking missile...

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小子}你别走啊！
t_en: Hey! Don't go, {F#m'girl}{M#boy}!

t_ch: 煞火。
t_en: Burnie.

t_ch: 这世道真是变了，现在的人活得实在毫无乐趣……
t_en: This world really has changed. People nowadays have no joy in their lives...

t_ch: 你在做自我介绍？
t_en: Was that a self-introduction?

t_ch: 老子凭什么要做自我介绍？给你们人类惯的，等等，你怎么骂人呢。
t_en: Why should I do that? You humans are all spoiled brats. Wait, were you cursing me out?

t_ch: 展开讲讲…
t_en: Go on, tell me more...

t_ch: 这还用我展开讲讲吗？你不就生活在这么个无趣的世界里吗？
t_en: Do I really need to say any more? You are already living in this joyless world.

t_ch: 我逃出来的时候，有阵子附在一个从来不出门的家伙身上，这家伙不是在网络上挑衅别人，就是威胁着要把对方真实身份开了盒……
t_en: When I escaped, I took possession for a while of this guy who never went outside. If he wasn't flaming people online, he was threatening to dox others...

t_ch: 老子一开始陪他玩的起劲，但时间久了，也是被这厮的戾气辣到吃不消了，就寻思换换口味，尝些清淡点的人类
t_en: At first, I had great fun playing together with him. But as time went by, I got a bit choked with that brat's salty spite and couldn't stand it any longer. So, I decided it was time to change flavors to a more bland variety of human.

t_ch: 结果下一个家伙可好，是个计程星槎士，我见他从早干到晚，没见他从舵手的位置上挪开步子……
t_en: Little did I know the next guy turned out to be a starskiff taxi pilot. He worked from dawn to dusk, and I never saw him taking a single step away from the helm...

t_ch: 我本想撺掇他偷个懒，结果这厮意志力比老子还顽强，还想让老子控制他的身体替他开船……
t_en: I wanted to push him into slacking off just a little, but this guy's willpower turned out to be even stronger than mine, and he even wanted me to control his body to steer for him...

t_ch: 像话吗！给他当开船苦力？老子是来享受你们人类纵情极欲的快乐的，是来给你打工的吗？
t_en: Seriously? Work as a driver for him? I'm here to gorge myself in the ultimate pleasures you humans feel when you give in to your desires, not to be a salary worker for you!

t_ch: 之后我为了去去晦气，就找了个废宅躲了起来。结果又遇到这档子事，哼哼……
t_en: Then, to shake off my bad luck, I found an old abandoned residence to hide in. And then I ran into this mess *groan*...

t_ch: 故事听的开心吧？开心的话就别再来烦我！！
t_en: Are you enjoying the story so far? If you are, then don't come back and bug me ever again!

t_ch: 煨赫。
t_en: Singerd.

t_ch: 喂，你，人类，你还记得我不？
t_en: Hello there, you, human. Do you still remember me?

t_ch: 你谁？
t_en: Who are you again?

t_ch: 哇，人类的脑子真不记事。
t_en: Wow, the human brain really is forgetful.

t_ch: 我和妖物不熟。
t_en: I'm not friends with monsters.

t_ch: 也是，你是给十王司干活的人，没有理由记得我。
t_en: True. You also work for the Ten-Lords Commission, so there's no reason for you to remember me.

t_ch: 说起来有些不好意思，我就是那个被困在魔阴身云骑身体里的岁阳。
t_en: It's a little embarrassing, but I'm the heliobus trapped inside the mara-struck Cloud Knight's body.

t_ch: 虽然那位小判官最后还是把我扔进了这里，但谢天谢地好过被困在一个疯狂的身体里。
t_en: Even though that little judge tossed me in here at the end, it's still thankfully better than being inside that crazy body.

t_ch: 那种感觉…打个你们人类能懂的比方，就是和一个有暴力倾向的精神失常者一起被反锁在一个五平米的单间里。
t_en: That feeling... to use an analogy you humans might understand, it was like being locked inside a five-by-five single cell with a mentally disturbed person with violent tendencies.

t_ch: 这样的生不如死的感觉，你能理解了吧？
t_en: That kind of fate is worse than death... can you understand it?

t_ch: 看起来仙舟人不适合你们…
t_en: Looks like Xianzhou humans aren't you heliobi's type....

t_ch: 岂止仙舟人，长生种的欲望全都难以消化。这些家伙……
t_en: Not just Xianzhou humans, but the desires of all long-life species are tough to digest. These people...

t_ch: 他们吃一样的食物、看一样的风景、在一样的工作岗位上加班几百年升职无望。
t_en: They eat the same food, view the same sights, and work the same jobs for centuries — with no hope of any promotion.

t_ch: 岁阳喜欢新鲜激烈的欲望。但长生种的念头，却像放陈了几百年的饼，又硬又干像块石头。真不知道他们一路是怎么过来的……
t_en: Heliobi love fresh, intense desires. But long-life species... their thoughts are like biscuits dried out in the sun for a hundred years. It's all hard and dry like a stone. I really have no idea how they've even made it this far in life...

t_ch: 离开镇妖塔。
t_en: Leave the suppression tower.

t_ch: 镇妖塔轻轻浮起，你想要与哪位岁阳对话？
t_en: The suppression towers float gently. Which heliobus would you like to talk to?

t_ch: 焱攸。
t_en: Immola.

t_ch: …呜呜。
t_en: ...Wooo.

t_ch: 这是岁阳能发出来的声音？
t_en: Is that a sound that heliobi can make?

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yup.

t_ch: 岁阳也有自由呜呜的权利。
t_en: Heliobi have the right to say wooo whenever we want.

t_ch: …呜呜伯？
t_en: ...Wubbaboo?

t_ch: 我好想成为呜呜伯，我怎么就不是呜呜伯……
t_en: I want to become a Wubbaboo. Oh, why am I not yet a Wubbaboo...

t_ch: …你不认识我，我也不认识你，我是被浮烟派去附身于十王司判官的…岁阳，呜呜……
t_en: ...You don't know me and I don't know you. I'm a... heliobus sent by Cirrus to take possession of a Ten-Lords Judge... Wooo *sob*...

t_ch: 我怕死了…特别是尾巴大爷叫我的时候，我已经想好下辈子重生的事了……
t_en: I'm scared to death... especially when Mr. Tail called me. I had already started planning out my post-rebirth life...

t_ch: 你们岁阳不是没法被消灭吗？
t_en: I thought you heliobi can never be destroyed.

t_ch: …请问您是在以消灭我为前提在与我讨论吗？
t_en: ...Excuse me. Are you only asking to find out how to destroy me?

t_ch: 你好可怕。
t_en: You're scary.

t_ch: 你们岁阳还有重生的说法吗？
t_en: You heliobi have rebirths?

t_ch: …我也不知道，没有岁阳研究过啊…难道你想说「没有」？
t_en: ...I don't know either. No heliobi has ever studied it before... Are you implying there is no such thing?

t_ch: 你好可怕。
t_en: You're scary.

t_ch: 不过附身那位判官的时候，我觉得我和她有着完美的适配性，我一度觉得我就是她，她就是我…但是走入她内心后，我觉得不是这样的。
t_en: However, when I possessed that judge, I could feel that she and I were perfectly compatible. I even thought I was her and her me... but after going deep into her heart, I learned that wasn't the case.

t_ch: 她虽然在害怕，但她想改变自己，想变得让人可以依靠…但是这哪有那么容易呢？我做不到付出这样的努力，我很羡慕她。
t_en: Despite being scared, she still wants to change herself and become somebody reliable... but how could that be so simple? I would never be able to make the effort required for such a change. I envy her.

t_ch: 不说了，我要回去了，再见。
t_en: Enough talk. I'm going back. So long.

t_ch: 销金。
t_en: Giltun.

t_ch: 人类，求我所为何事？是觉得自己错过了发财的机会，后悔了吗？
t_en: Human, what do you seek from me? Worried you've missed out on a chance to get rich, and you are starting to regret it?

t_ch: 我告诉你，来不及了！机会从来只会留给有准备的人！不过，你要是肯放我出去……
t_en: I'm telling you, time's running out! Chances only come to those who are prepared for it! However, if you're willing to let me out of here, then...

t_ch: 随便聊聊…
t_en: Just a little chit-chat...

t_ch: 随便？聊聊？我的时间非常值钱，而我只为那些愿意追逐财富、渴望成就自己的年轻人提供金子般宝贵的建议！
t_en: Little chit-chat? My time is extremely valuable, and I only dole out my precious gold-plated advice to those youngsters with the thirst and desire to chase down wealth and personal achievements!

t_ch: 我代表十王司来审问你…
t_en: I am here on behalf of the Ten-Lords Commission to interrogate you...

t_ch: 哼，落到你们手里，我无话可说。但别指望我作为你的财富顾问，给你出谋划策！
t_en: Hmph, I've fallen into you people's hands and have nothing to say. But don't expect me to give you strategic advice as some kind of financial advisor!

t_ch: 你这岁阳怎么张口就是钱？
t_en: You're a heliobus. Why are you always on about money?

t_ch: 那当然是因为…呃，当然是因为，因为你们人类对金钱的热爱感动了我。
t_en: Well, of course that's because... er, of course it's because... because I found the human love for money to be touching.

t_ch: 你是资本主义岁阳？
t_en: Are you a capitalistic heliobus?

t_ch: 资本主义…我听过这个词。但那不过是你们人类给金钱力量套上的无聊概念之一罢了。
t_en: Capitalistic... I've heard of that word before. But that's just one of those boring concepts you humans wrap around the power of money.

t_ch: 是的，身为岁阳，我确实无处花销金钱——但每当你们人类获得金钱后体内涌出的那股幸福感，品尝起来就好像是浓稠的蜜糖，实在美味！
t_en: Yes, as a heliobus, I have no real capacity for spending money — but every time you humans get hold of money, the happiness that gushes out of you tastes like rich honey — so sweet!

t_ch: 不得不承认，岁阳固然能随心幻化，但相比人类的金钱置换物质的奇妙力量，还是稍逊一筹。
t_en: I have to admit that, despite heliobi being able to create illusions at will, such powers are still slightly inferior to the miraculous human ability to exchange currency for possessions.

t_ch: 我曾经帮助一个被父母卖往他乡的孤儿攀上公司分部主管的位置，想看看他会如何使用到手的金钱……
t_en: I once helped an orphan, who was sold off by his parents to a foreign land, to rise to the position of an IPC branch manager. I wanted to see how he'd use the money he had...

t_ch: 他拼命压榨手下的员工没日没夜干了几年，最后把公司给他的奖金买下一颗小行星。
t_en: He worked his employees to the brink of death for a few years, then used his IPC bonus to buy an asteroid.

t_ch: 我本以为那会是某个浪漫约定的开始，谁能想到他竟然让这颗小行星的轨道偏转了几度，让它在六个月后坠落在了自己的故乡……
t_en: I thought it would be the beginning of some romantic promise, but who knew that he would make the asteroid's orbit shift by a few degrees, so it would plummet into his home world six months later...

t_ch: 我曾经帮助一名身患绝症的销售成功卖出了他们世界的太阳，治好了他所在世界的科技绝不可能治好的顽疾。
t_en: I once helped a terminally-ill salesman sell off his planet's sun to cure his disease, which was absolutely incurable within the technological limitations of his own world.

t_ch: 但很可惜，他那幼稚的女儿在得知了这件事后对他大加责怪，于是这个脆弱的家伙用一根绳子结束了自己的性命。
t_en: Unfortunately, his naive daughter remonstrated him upon learning of the incident, so the weak guy ended his life with a length of rope.

t_ch: 人们为了钱出卖自己的生命，爱情和忠诚；又用钱买下他们本不可能买到的时间、快乐和死亡……在这个过程里，我见过太多太多奇妙的事情。
t_en: People sell off their life, love, and loyalty for money. Then, they use that money to buy the time, happiness, and death that they could never have afforded before... I have witnessed many miraculous things throughout this process.

t_ch: 要让岁阳来办成这些事，那得花上数不尽的能量。可金钱只是转手便做到了。我怎么能不爱死这种颠来倒去的感觉呢！
t_en: If heliobi were to try and do this, it would require vast amounts of energy, but money achieves this simply by changing hands. How could I not be obsessed with this process of something bouncing through owners like this?

t_ch: 所以，你想不想跟着我发一笔横财，{F#小妹妹}{M#小子}？
t_en: So then, would you like to hit the jackpot with me, {F#young lady}{M#young man}?

t_ch: 宵炽。
t_en: Dawnyng.

t_ch: 吼吼…居然主动靠近了吗，不选择逃跑，而是主动向我宵炽靠近吗？
t_en: Ohoho... You're actually taking the initiative to get close to me. Instead of choosing to flee, you're approaching me?

t_ch: 你谁啊？我们之前有说过话吗？
t_en: Who are you? Have we ever meet before?

t_ch: 我们怎么没有说过话了？
t_en: What do you mean we haven't met before?

t_ch: 你们拎着一个鸟笼来鳞渊境，是我挡在了你们面前！
t_en: You people came to the Scalegorge Waterscape once with a birdcage, and I was the one who blocked your path!

t_ch: 要不走近点，怎么把你揍趴下。
t_en: If I don't come closer, how am I supposed to beat you down?

t_ch: 你小子还挺狂啊。要不是我被囚禁在这镇妖塔中，我可要对你大打出手了。
t_en: You talk pretty big, kid. If I weren't imprisoned in this suppression tower, I would have already punched you.

t_ch: …听你这话
t_en: ...Hearing you say this...

t_ch: …让我品一品，嗯…你的味道有些苦涩，就好像是痛苦回忆而无半点结果——看来你真的不记得我，我就这么没有存在感吗？
t_en: ...Let me have a taste, um... Your flavor is a little astringent, like you're trying hard to remember but can't recall anything — it seems like you really have no memory of me. Do I really make such a small impression?

t_ch: 不过，我也不在乎！我是一个特立独行的岁阳，大家都说待在魔阴身里难受，我偏要待在魔阴身那里！在魔阴身的意识中越痛苦，我就会越强大。
t_en: But then again, I don't care! I'm a heliobus who marches at my own beat. Everyone says staying inside a mara-struck body feels horrible, but I want to do exactly that! The more painful I am in the mara-struck's conscious, the more powerful I'll become.

t_ch: 杀不死我的，只能让我更强大！
t_en: What doesn't kill me will only make me stronger!

t_ch: 没想到你还有这样的爱好。
t_en: I never thought you have such a hobby.

t_ch: 你误会了，受苦不是我的爱好，变强才是我的爱好。
t_en: Make no mistake, suffering is not my hobby. Getting powerful is my hobby.

t_ch: 那你真的变强大了吗？
t_en: So, did you really get more powerful?

t_ch: 我觉得多半是有变强大吧…不然我的苦就白吃了。
t_en: I think I've gotten more powerful... otherwise, my suffering would have been all for nothing.

t_ch: 我在魔阴身里感受到一种接近于疯狂的生命力，我想想该如何解释于你，就像是野兽之于人类，他们遵循直觉行事，以生存为第一准则，超越了道德伦理。
t_en: While inside the mara-struck body, I felt a vitality that is close to madness. How can I explain it to you — just like beasts are to humans, the mara-struck acts according to intuition and prioritizes their own survival over morals and ethics.

t_ch: 而魔阴身的疯狂之于野兽，更胜于野兽之于人类…它是一种更加本源的生命力，抛弃血肉形态的充分生长，光是想想就让我感觉到痛苦。
t_en: But the difference between a mara-struck body's madness and that of a beast is even greater than the difference between a beast and a human... It's a more primitive form of life, a vitality that discards even the concept of corporeal existence. Just thinking about it hurts me.

t_ch: …不行了，我的意识开始模糊，再继续回想下去我可能会裂开变成分灵了。
t_en: ...No. That's too much. My consciousness is flagging. If I keep thinking about it, I might split up into smaller heliobi.

t_ch: …不跟你聊了，回见。
t_en: ...That's enough talking. Goodbye.

t_ch: 宝烛。
t_en: Arkeri.

t_ch: 我收到了三月七给我的照片。
t_en: I received the photo from March 7th.

t_ch: 那是一座太空站吗？我看到了星空和站台。
t_en: Is that a space station? I can see stars and a station platform.

t_ch: 那是「黑塔」空间站。
t_en: That's the Herta Space Station.

t_ch: 原来是叫这个名字吗，我记住了。
t_en: So that's what it's called. I'll remember it.

t_ch: 那里有着各种各样的奇物。
t_en: There're all kinds of diverse Curios over there.

t_ch: 奇物？那里也有岁阳吗？
t_en: Curios? Do they have heliobi there, too?

t_ch: 那里有我认识的好朋友。
t_en: I have good friends there.

t_ch: 那可真是太好了。
t_en: That's great.

t_ch: 抱歉，印象不深了。
t_en: I'm sorry. I can't remember much about it anymore.

t_ch: 没关系，我会自己想象。
t_en: That's alright. I'll just imagine them myself.

t_ch: 还有还有，这个白雪皑皑的世界好有意思。
t_en: Also, this snow-capped world is really something.

t_ch: 我在那里被通缉了。
t_en: I was a wanted suspect there.

t_ch: 是吗？那可真是惊险呀。
t_en: Really? That sounds thrilling.

t_ch: 我在那里受到大守护者的接待。
t_en: I was welcomed by the Supreme Guardian there.

t_ch: 我不知道「大守护者」意味着什么，肯定是了不起的人吧。
t_en: I don't know what "Supreme Guardian" means, but it sounds like a pretty awesome person.

t_ch: 我在那里认识了许多的朋友。
t_en: I have made many friends there.

t_ch: 您肯定无论到哪里都能结识许多朋友吧。
t_en: I have faith that you will make many friends wherever you go.

t_ch: 往事已经记不得了。
t_en: I can't remember much about the past.

t_ch: 嗯，您得有多少丰富的经历才会轻描淡写地说出这句话呀。
t_en: For you to downplay things like that... you must really have a huge wealth of experiences behind you.

t_ch: 三月七的照片真是太好了。
t_en: This photo from March 7th is truly beautiful.

t_ch: 你也许还是应该回归天外。
t_en: Maybe you should go back beyond the sky.

t_ch: 感谢您的好心，不过您不必担心。
t_en: Thanks for your concern, but don't worry about it.

t_ch: 看着照片就满足了吗？
t_en: Are you happy just looking at the photo?

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我会陷入休眠，而当我醒来，照片里的世界会陪伴我。
t_en: Mmm, I'll fall asleep and when I wake up, the world in the photo will be with me.

t_ch: 尽管我对天外世界充满了期望，但是我还没有做好准备，要是就这样出发，肯定还会像上次一样中途夭折。
t_en: Despite being brimming with expectations of the worlds beyond the sky, I'm not ready yet. If I go off like this, I'll definitely run into a crisis midway just like last time.

t_ch: 我想做好完全准备之后，再离开这里。感谢您和我聊天，再见。
t_en: I want to be completely prepared before I leave here again. Thank you for chatting, and see you later.

t_ch: 离开镇妖塔。
t_en: Leave the suppression tower.

t_ch: 镇妖塔轻轻浮起，浮烟不耐烦地回应了你的呼唤。
t_en: The suppression tower floats gently. Cirrus responds impatiently to your call.

t_ch: 小虫子，别来骚扰我！
t_en: Gnat! Quit bugging me!

t_ch: 想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 你想问就问，我爱答不答。
t_en: Ask me whatever you want, and I'll answer if I feel like it.

t_ch: 腾骁将军是个什么样的人？
t_en: What kind of person was General Teng Xiao?

t_ch: 你问对人了。燎原的碎片们散落已久，关于腾骁的记忆，许多岁阳早已模糊不清了。但唯独我还记得……
t_en: You're asking the right person. Ignamar's fragments have long been scattered, and Teng Xiao's memory is fuzzy for many heliobi. Yet I remain, the only one who remembers everything...

t_ch: 他和如今这个…神策将军，完全没有一丝相像的地方。他是个纯粹的武人，来去直截了当。不知他为何竟选了这么一个奸猾狡诈的继任者……
t_en: There's absolutely nothing in common between him and this current... Divine Foresight. He is a pure warrior, blunt and straightforward in every way. Who knows why he'd choose a successor as crafty and cunning as this one...

t_ch: 接下来有什么逃脱计划吗？
t_en: Any further escape plans?

t_ch: 有有有，来，你让我附身在你身上，我偷偷讲给你听听？
t_en: Yes, yes, yes I do. Come and let me possess you, and then I can whisper it in your ears. Deal?

t_ch: 其实我心里有个成型的计划，不过缺的既不是工具、也不是人手，而是时间——需要至少一个琥珀纪来准备。
t_en: I've actually got a complete plan in mind. I'm lacking in neither the tools nor manpower to complete it, but time — I need at least one Amber Era to get things ready.

t_ch: 我寻思你应该看不到那时候了…也许景元能看到，也许他也看不到了。但无论如何，仙舟将会看到，哼哼哼……
t_en: I reckon you won't be able to see it with your own eyes... Maybe Jing Yuan would, or maybe he wouldn't. But no matter what, the Xianzhou will definitely witness it. Hahaha...

t_ch: 你能给我表演个才艺吗？
t_en: Can you show me some tricks?

t_ch: 尽情嘲笑吧，讽刺吧！你对我的侮辱，在我下次降临时，我会加倍偿还在无名客和仙舟之上……
t_en: Fine! Laugh at me! Mock me! The next time I descend upon this world, I will repay your insults tenfold upon the Nameless and the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 真希望你能活久一些，见证我的复仇啊，小虫子。
t_en: I truly hope you can live long enough to witness my revenge, you tiny gnat.

t_ch: 不聊了，再见。
t_en: Enough talk. Bye.

t_ch: 滚一边去，别烦我。
t_en: Get lost and don't bug me again.

t_ch: 不聊了，再见。
t_en: Enough talk. Bye.

t_ch: 滚一边去，别烦我。
t_en: Get lost and don't bug me again.

t_ch: 很好很好，宝贝儿，刚才你的招式确实让我全身都感到爽快！
t_en: Very good, my dear. Your refreshingly thrilling move jolted me right up!

t_ch: 哦～你还有什么真本事，能不能再对着我的脑袋来这么一下，让我好好玩玩～
t_en: Oh, do you even have any real skill? Can you give my head another hit so I can have some fun?

t_ch: 刚才的招式还不够厉害？
t_en: Are my moves not impressive enough?

t_ch: 刚才那一下子还不错，不过力道稍微差了点，换言之，还不够！
t_en: That last hit was decent, but it was lacking power. In other words, it's not enough!

t_ch: 说实话，你让我有点恶心。
t_en: Honestly, you're grossing me out.

t_ch: 真是的，现在的人还真是少见多怪。公共场合的木人桩能有什么恶心的？
t_en: Honestly, people are so easily shook nowadays. What's so disgusting about a wooden dummy in a public area?

t_ch: 你仔细看看我，我从不抱怨，反而享受其中…和旁边那些毫无特色的{RUBY_B#公共木人桩}货色{RUBY_E#}可完全不一样，{F#壮小妞}{M#壮小伙}你可得好好记住哟～
t_en: Take a close look at me: I never grumble, I revel in it... I'm totally different from those mundane {RUBY_B#public wooden dummies}crappy dummies{RUBY_E#}. {F#Strong lass}{M#Strong lad}, you'd do best to remember that!

t_ch: 不过嘛，你也真是蛮特别的，刚才那一招，唔…让我再回味一下——强硬的力道，刁钻的角度，以及狠辣的技巧，简直是让我一眼就爱上的程度。
t_en: Though, you're pretty special. That move just now... Mmm, let me recall it again: A powerful stroke, a precise angle, ferocious technique, the kind that would make me fall in love at first sight.

t_ch: 要是你能给我展示你全部的真本事，我敢打保票，你绝对有资格成为竞锋舰上的胜者。
t_en: If you could demonstrate your full repertoire, I guarantee you'll be the undisputed champion of the Skysplitter.

t_ch: 将来你会有机会感受到的。
t_en: You'll get a chance to experience it first-hand in the future.

t_ch: 哈哈，亲爱的，我就吃你这款，真是舍不得让你离开了。
t_en: Haha, my dear, you're just my type. I really can't bear to let you go.

t_ch: 我是不会对你使出全力的。
t_en: I'll never unleash my full might on you.

t_ch: 你好坏啊，你这种不听话又霸道的{F#女人}{M#男人}最对我的胃口了。
t_en: You're so bad! A {F#woman}{M#man} as sassy and incorrigible like you has me absolutely smitten.

t_ch: 再告诉你个小秘密好了…你再贴近一些……
t_en: I'll let you in on a little secret... Scoot closer just a little...

t_ch: 你就在这个距离说吧。
t_en: You can say it from where you are.

t_ch: 哎呀，别犟嘛～算了，俗话说距离也能产生美，你真的好美，我原谅你了。
t_en: Oh c'mon, don't play coy... Forget it, they don't say "beauty creates distance" for nothing. You're really beautiful, so I'll forgive you.

t_ch: （适当地贴近一些）
t_en: (Scoot closer appropriately)

t_ch: 宝贝儿，我就喜欢跟这种紧贴着的感觉～毕竟我走不了的，只有等你贴近了。
t_en: My dear, I love nothing more than the feeling of such intimate quarters... Considering I can't move, so all I can do is wait for you to come closer.

t_ch: 这些话，就请当作是我们俩的秘密。我呀，悄悄记住了竞锋舰上所有造访的选手，以及他们擅长的招式还有的弱点，无一例外，我都可以告诉你。
t_en: Let my next words be our little secret: I've surreptitiously memorized all the visiting contenders on the Skysplitter, along with their trademark moves and their weaknesses, all of them. And I'll tell you everything.

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍了，请叫我「包打听」——不仅包打，还包打听，很难忘记吧？
t_en: I forgot to introduce myself, call me Bao Dating — not just prolific at being punched, but also at acquiring intel. I'm good, no?

t_ch: 唉？不继续打我了吗？你可千万不要因为我是娇花而怜惜我！
t_en: Eh? You've stopped hitting me? Don't stop on account of me being a delicate little flower!

t_ch: 有些事想问问你。
t_en: I have some stuff to ask you.

t_ch: 好的，宝贝～别说你想问什么，就是想做什么也都可以哦～
t_en: Sure thing, my dear! Ask me anything, or even better, do everything to me!

t_ch: 你怎么知道他们的招数？
t_en: How did you learn of their moves?

t_ch: 这是咱的工作嘛～人家其实是「自主学习反馈型训练偃偶」～凡是跟人家深入交流过的选手，他们的一招一式，一举一动，我全都了然于心～
t_en: That's just our job! I'm a self-learning feedback training puppet. Once I've intimately exchanged moves with any fighter, I'll naturally learn of their every move, every step, every stroke!

t_ch: 毕竟人家用身体真真切切地感受到他们的招数，就能用数据化的方式记录下动量、力矩和频次，还能根据数据分析提出招式的改进意见～
t_en: I mean, I did use my own body to personally experience their skills, allowing me to record the momentum, torque, and frequency of each and every one of their moves with data-driven methods. I can even analyze and provide suggestions to improve their skills.

t_ch: 不过，人家和那些只会上班填数据的{RUBY_B#公共木人桩}货色{RUBY_E#}不一样，我身上装着痛觉模拟器，有着更为真切的打击反馈。
t_en: But, I'm obviously different from those {RUBY_B#public wooden dummy}data pushers{RUBY_E#} who just clock in. I'm outfitted with pain simulators that can receive impact feedback more realistically.

t_ch: 所以说，寻常的动手动脚可满足不了我，你可得用点劲狠狠地打我～
t_en: Which is why ordinary fondling won't ever be able to satisfy my thirst — you have to hit me really, really hard!

t_ch: 你这样做就不怕被打坏？
t_en: You're not afraid of being damaged?

t_ch: 还有这好事？求求你把人家打坏了啦～
t_en: I yearn for such fortune! Please, hit me till I break!

t_ch: 毕竟我是偃偶，没有你们这些有机生物潜藏在基因里的「求生欲」，你要是狠狠打我呢，只要没把我打坏，我都不会感到害怕，只会感到兴奋！
t_en: I'm a puppet, after all, devoid of any "survival instinct" present in organic lifeforms like you. You can smash me all you like, and as long as you don't break me, I won't feel an ounce of fear, only thrill!

t_ch: 等你真正将我打坏以后，我就什么也感觉不到了。我这样解释，你就明白这其中的原理了吧？不过，你这么担心我，我向你保证，我会活得好好的～
t_en: And when you really break me, I won't feel a thing. You understand the logic from my explanation? Though, since you're so worried about me, I promise to live my best life!

t_ch: 你可别小看了人家，就这竞锋舰上的选手，还没几个能伤到我的～若没有个好躯体，怎么去切实感受这些火辣辣的力量呢～
t_en: You shouldn't underestimate me, though — there are few contenders who can even dent me aboard the Skysplitter. How would I be able to profoundly experience spicy, exhilarating strength without a tenacious physique?

t_ch: 有什么可以打听的情报吗？
t_en: Have you heard anything useful for me?

t_ch: 看在你先前打的那么用力的份上，让人家想想……
t_en: Mm, you certainly pummeled me hard enough before, so let me think...

t_ch: 最值得注意的选手嘛，不就是经常和你在一起的那位小小的云骑骁卫咯～
t_en: The fighter most worthy of attention, is obviously that Cloud Knight Lieutenant who's always hanging around you!

t_ch: 那位小朋友还真的挺特别，每次找人家「切磋」时，都感觉招数有着些许的变化。我还记得他那一招凛冽凌厉的剑法，冰得人家瑟瑟发抖～
t_en: That little scamp is pretty special. Every time he spars with me, I can feel like shifts in his moves. I still remember that cold, merciless sword move of his — it sent shivers down my spine (if I had one)!

t_ch: 如果再给他几年，说不定会出落成一个帅小伙…噢不是，我是说他会成为一名顶尖的剑术高手～啊～希望那时他还能想起我，找我试试剑……
t_en: Give him another few years, and he'll be a total showstopper... I mean, a stunningly exceptional swordsman! Oooh, I hope he will still remember me then, and hit me up to try out his piercing sword moves...

t_ch: 看在你先前打的那么用力的份上，让人家想想…
t_en: Mm, you certainly pummeled me hard enough before, so let me think...

t_ch: 有一位异星的选手，倒是让人家印象深刻。虽然不知道他的名字，但那头红发…尤其那壮硕的金属义肢让人家铭心刻骨。
t_en: There is an otherworld fighter who left a deep impression on me. I don't know his name, but that head of red hair... and especially that rock-hard arm of metal are seared in my mind.

t_ch: 他走位灵动，快拳像疾风骤雨一般吹进人家的心里。啊。那一拳一拳的咚咚声，是人家狂乱的内心在小鹿乱撞～
t_en: He moved with finesse, his swift punches were like a storm that blew their way into my heart. Ah... the clangs of that fist, that was my heart chaotically beating aflutter...

t_ch: 不过他的招数看起来像是野路子，感觉就像是找了个退伍老兵练过段时间。
t_en: But his moves looked like he was self-taught, like he trained under some discharged veteran.

t_ch: 很少选手都能像他那样自律，能和他一起见到凌晨四点的竞锋舰，实在是浪漫极了。我敢说，这份决心和毅力是他最大的武器～
t_en: Very few contenders are as disciplined as him. To be able to admire the Skysplitter in the wee hours of the morning with him, there's nothing more romantic than that. I'd dare say that this resolve and determination of his are his greatest weapons.

t_ch: 看在你先前打的那么用力的份上，让人家想想…
t_en: Mm, you certainly pummeled me hard enough before, so let me think...

t_ch: 确实有这么一位异星的选手，他和所有的人都不一样～枪快人更快，似乎从小就学会如何与土匪搏斗，如何与野兽争夺空间，如何为自己赢得尊重。
t_en: There is an outworlder who's different from everyone else. His gun's quick, but his moves are even quicker! Like as soon as he learned how to walk, he practiced fighting bandits, how to vie for territory with beasts, how to win respect for himself.

t_ch: 每一枪都射中人家的玉兆模块。就像是在人家的心上凿开一个洞……
t_en: His every shot hit my jade abacus. Like he was boring a hole into my heart...

t_ch: 他满口花言巧语…一口一句「宝贝」，一口一句「爱死」～这是什么新式表白嘛？不过嘛，和人家倒是般配。
t_en: He was really silver-tongued too... calling me "muddle-fudger" and "son of a nice lady" all the time. Is this some new form of confession? But then again, he's really suited to me.

t_ch: 看在你先前打的那么用力的份上，让人家想想…
t_en: Mm, you certainly pummeled me hard enough before, so let me think...

t_ch: 先前倒是见到了一位朱明穿着的小姑娘，扛着一柄比她还大的剑呢！不过好可惜啊～那个小姑娘似乎对人家没什么兴趣。
t_en: I met a little lady in Zhuming garb recently who was carrying a sword that towered over her! It was a pity, though, that she wasn't interested in hitting me.

t_ch: 唉，人家好想被那柄浑厚的大剑粗暴地修理一番～
t_en: *sigh* And I was looking forward to being taught a lesson by that heap of raw iron~

t_ch: 看在你先前打的那么用力的份上，让人家想想…
t_en: Mm, you certainly pummeled me hard enough before, so let me think...

t_ch: 人家想起了一位异星的美男子，他身着一身闪耀瑰丽的银色盔甲，有着一头柔顺飘逸的红色秀发～
t_en: I'm reminded of a dashing outworlder gentleman who wore a suit of scintillating silver armor, with a full head of silky-smooth, crimson hair!

t_ch: 他挥舞长枪，举止优雅，招式华丽。最关键的是他从一群木人桩里，一眼相中了人家，全是发自内心地赞美！天呐，人家简直是受宠若惊！
t_en: He wielded a long spear, his poise was elegant, and his moves brimming with splendor. Most importantly, he spotted me among a group of wooden dummies — his eyes were full of heartfelt admiration! Heavens, I felt simply pampered!

t_ch: 要是能他用长枪多打打我就好了，毕竟在我心中，兵刃相接的声音的比任何的赞美声都更加悦耳。
t_en: It would've been perfect if he stabbed me real good with that spear of his. I mean, the sound of weaponry impacting my body is more music to my ears than any praise.

t_ch: 没别的事，告辞。
t_en: I'm all done, goodbye.

t_ch: 好嘛～记得常来找人家「切磋」啊～
t_en: Okay, don't forget to visit me for more sparring!

t_ch: 没别的事了，告辞
t_en: I'm all done, farewell.

t_ch: 好嘛～记得常来找人家「切磋」啊～
t_en: Okay, don't forget to visit me for more sparring!

t_ch: 快来打我呀…
t_en: Hurry up and hit me...

t_ch: 我特别欠打…
t_en: I've been aching for a good beating...

t_ch: 打我！求求你了！
t_en: Hit me! Please!

t_ch: 不要因为我是娇花而怜惜我！
t_en: Don't look down on me just because I'm a delicate little flower!

t_ch: 使点劲，宝贝，你打不坏我的！
t_en: Put some effort into it, darling. You can't break me!

t_ch: 用点力！人家吃的住～
t_en: Use more force! I can take it!

t_ch: 力道够了，准度差了点～
t_en: You're using enough strength, but your precision needs work!

t_ch: 小师傅，要切中路！
t_en: Warrior, you have to strike with accuracy!

t_ch: 很好，尽情释放招式！
t_en: Very good, unleash your best moves all over me!

t_ch: 核心发力！就是这感觉！
t_en: Exert your strength from your core! That's it, feel it!

t_ch: 嗯？是新面孔，又是哪里来的新人选手么？有什么不懂的多问问我这位老前辈啊。我会让你知难而退…啊，不对，我会让你明白竞锋舰上的赛程有多艰辛的。
t_en: Hmm? A new face, another new participant from somewhere? You can count on this old veteran if you have any questions. I'll have you running off in no time... Ah, that's not right — I'll make you understand the ardor of the Skysplitter's tournament.

t_ch: 简而言之，所有新人参加比赛都会遇到一道坎。我，沧汐，就是那道坎。
t_en: Simply put, all newcomers will eventually hit a wall in this contest. I, Cangxi, am that wall.

t_ch: 你还蛮骄傲自豪的哦？
t_en: You're pretty smug, aren't you?

t_ch: 我太骄傲了。
t_en: I'm overly smug.

t_ch: 别以为我听不出你语气中夹枪带棒，我不在乎，我就是骄傲～
t_en: Don't think I can't sense the sarcasm dripping from your words, not that I care. Smug is my middle name.

t_ch: 老前辈？难道你很老么？
t_en: Old veteran? Are you really that old?

t_ch: 我参加过的比赛年头，可比你们短生种一辈子的时间都多。
t_en: I've joined more competitions than you short-life species could ever experience in your lifetime.

t_ch: 你看我像是参加比赛的吗？
t_en: You think I'm here to participate in the tournament?

t_ch: 我看你…像是神策府的贵客。对不起，是我说话太大声了。
t_en: I think you... look like an esteemed guest of the Seat of Divine Foresight. My apologies, I was too loud.

t_ch: 曾经有人这样评价我——「沧汐从不考虑赢下冠军，只管碾压新人。」
t_en: I was told this once by someone: "Cangxi has never coveted the champion's title, only the crushing of newbies."

t_ch: 我认为这样的评价很准确。别看我常年混迹于业余选手、赛场新人之间，好像也是新人。但不才已经坚持击败新人上百年了。江湖抬爱，称我为「新人杀手」！
t_en: I think that is a fair assessment. I may seem like a hobbyist contender, a compatriot among newcomers. But I have persevered in beating down newcomers for centuries. My beloved moniker by the community is "Newbie Killer"!

t_ch: 难道不是因为你无法晋级？
t_en: It's not because you can't get ahead to the next level?

t_ch: 如果只以为我是无法晋级那就大错特错了。不是我谦虚，以我的实力早就可以晋级。不过，这算是我的舒适区吧……
t_en: You're gravely mistaken if you think that's the case. I'm not trying to be modest, but I could've advanced long ago with my skills. It's just that this is my comfort zone...

t_ch: 这可不是什么好名声啊…
t_en: Doesn't sound like anything good...

t_ch: 你也许不想做「新人杀手」，但是这对于我来说，就是好名声！
t_en: You may not want to be the "Newbie Killer," but it's an awesome nickname to me!

t_ch: 不过，我这样做是有道理的。你想啊，你将新人时期放眼于整个选手的职业生涯中来看，是不是就好比人一生之中的青春期？
t_en: Of course, I have my reasons for doing this. Think about it: If you view the newcomer's phase as part of a contestant's entire career, isn't it like the adolescence in a person's life?

t_ch: 在青春期的时候，任何人都会遭遇困难与挫折，那些遗憾与悔恨，都将成为遥远未来的美好回忆。要我说啊，没有遗憾的青春才是最遗憾的！
t_en: When anyone is in their youth, they will undoubtedly run into trials and tribulations. Those experiences of regret and remorse will become beautiful memories in the distant future. Personally, spending your youth without regrets is the most regretful thing!

t_ch: 青春伤痛可不是欺负老实人啊！
t_en: The sorrow of youth shouldn't be used to bully the earnest!

t_ch: 我不知道你在说什么，我不欺负老实人，我只欺负新人。
t_en: I don't know what you're talking about. I'm not bullying decent folk, I'm just bullying newcomers.

t_ch: 青春可不是只能剩下遗憾啊！
t_en: You're not meant to leave behind regrets in your youth!

t_ch: 遇上我，他们就只能剩下遗憾了。
t_en: They'll have nothing else but regret when they run into me.

t_ch: 说这么好听，你只是想爽一下。
t_en: You make it sound so lofty, but you really just want to feel superior.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，看着新人们怀疑自身实力时露出的错愕沮丧神情…可真是太有意思了！
t_en: Heh-heh, the look of creeping depression of a newcomer doubting their own ability... It's simply addicting!

t_ch: 谁让他们技不如人的？菜就多练，输不起就别来。
t_en: They have no one else to blame for their inferiority. Train harder if you're crap, and get lost if you can't handle loss.

t_ch: 菜就多练，输不起就别来。这就是演武仪典的残酷！
t_en: Train harder if you're crap, and get lost if you can't handle loss. Such is the cold, brutal truth of the Wardance!

t_ch: 有些事想问你。
t_en: I have a few questions I wish to ask you...

t_ch: 别想劝我啊，我也有自己的坚持的。除此之外，你想问些什么？
t_en: Don't try to dissuade me, I've got my own principles too. Aside from what we just spoke about, What else do you want to ask?

t_ch: 你的武功是从哪学来的？
t_en: Where did you learn your martial arts?

t_ch: 这你就有所不知了，我们持明族是能蜕生轮回的。即使没法继承上一世的能力，但也可以记下自己的所学留给下一世。
t_en: I bet you don't know this: We Vidyadhara can be reborn through hatching rebirth. Even if we can't inherit our abilities from the previous cycle, we can still record what we've learned for the next cycle.

t_ch: 前世的「我」，那可是位武学大家。在「我」即将蜕生前，便将自己平生所学记录在册，其中也包含了呼吸吐纳，御气和拳脚的功夫。
t_en: The previous "me" was a master martial artist. Before "I" was reborn, I would record all I learned in a journal, including the skill of exhalation, Qi suppression and unarmed combat moves.

t_ch: 我按照前世笔记上面的方式修炼，果然武功大进。这么说吧，在武学这条路上，我是出生在起跑线的人。我学「我」自己，这可不算作弊吧？
t_en: I trained according to the instructions left behind by my previous self, and my martial arts inevitably improved. On the path of martial arts, I am someone who was born at the starting line. It's not considered cheating if I learn from myself, is it?

t_ch: 有什么值得注意的新人？
t_en: Any newcomers I should pay attention to?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，之前我确实遇到了一位棘手的新人，我输了，没能让他留下遗憾。如果说我是「新人杀手」，那么他就算是「专治老害」了。
t_en: To be honest, I did encounter a pretty troublesome newbie. I was defeated, and unable to leave any regrets for him. If I'm the Newbie Killer, that guy would probably be the "Veteran Slayer."

t_ch: 你听说过有位挑战彦卿骁卫的异星拳手叫卢卡吗？
t_en: You heard of Luka, that outworlder fighter who challenged Lieutenant Yanqing?

t_ch: 我是他的教练。
t_en: I'm his coach.

t_ch: 怪不得，他身上也有种强者的气息。
t_en: No wonder, he had the aura of a champion on him.

t_ch: 我…好像不认识！
t_en: I... don't think I do!

t_ch: 不认识就不认识嘛，不用好像。
t_en: You can just say you don't know him.

t_ch: 不过说起来，上一世的「我」虽强为武学大家，但是根据记录来看，他在演武仪典上遇到了一位红发化外民的选手，好像叫「伊戈尔」？
t_en: Speaking of which, while the previous "me" was an exceptional martial artist, but according to my records, they encountered a red-haired outworlder contestant in the Wardance, I think his name was Igor?

t_ch: 那个选手断了一臂，所用的是金属义肢。上一世的「我」认为传统功夫点到为止，打算先亮个相，结果那个化外民不讲武德。
t_en: That fellow was one-armed, replaced by a metallic prosthetic. The previous "me" was a strict observer of traditional martial etiquette, and wanted to take a photo first, but that outworlder was dishonorable.

t_ch: 比赛开始后，上一世的「我」还没摆完造型，就被他一记上勾拳直接打出赛场。致使上一世的「我」饮恨离世。
t_en: When the match started, before the previous "me" could even get his pose ready, he was sent flying out of the ring by a vicious uppercut. Because of this, the previous "me" died with a heart full of regret.

t_ch: 等等…红发、独臂、义肢…咱和咱的前世不会是遇到了什么半神的锋刃吧？
t_en: Hang on... Red-haired, one-armed, prosthetic... we couldn't have run into the metaphorical blade of a demigod, could we?

t_ch: 有什么对付新人的心得？
t_en: Have you any insights on fighting newcomers?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你问的好！
t_en: Hahaha, great question!

t_ch: 探听对手弱点，做好充足准备，这些其实都是次要的。
t_en: Find out about your opponent's weaknesses and make the utmost preparation — actually, this is secondary.

t_ch: 我只有一点心得需要你记住——不要手下留情。只要能做到这点，无论是新人还是老人，你都能将对方彻底击溃。
t_en: You only need to remember one rule — never, ever be merciful. As long as you can accomplish this, newbie or not, you'll be able to crush your opponent.

t_ch: 没别的事了，告辞。
t_en: I don't have anything to ask, goodbye.

t_ch: 没关系，想要击败新人的话，请随时来问我心得。
t_en: It's fine. You want to lay the hurt on any newbies, come to me for advice anytime.

t_ch: 没别的事了，告辞。
t_en: I don't have anything to ask, goodbye.

t_ch: 没关系，想要击败新人的话，请随时来问我心得。
t_en: It's fine. You want to lay the hurt on any newbies, come to me for advice anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，见到你真开心～今日来丹鼎司，难道说，你是有什么事要找妾身吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm so happy to see you~ Are you here at the Alchemy Commission today for me? What can I do for you?

t_ch: 想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I wanted to chat with you.

t_ch: 好呀。和你聊天不用在胸中做那许多忖度，正可以当成大脑按摩～
t_en: Certainly. I don't have to consider or speculate unnecessarily when I'm talking with you. It's relaxing, like a head massage~

t_ch: 最近工作还顺利吗？
t_en: Has work been going well lately?

t_ch: 哎…很难谈得上顺利。在这小小的丹鼎司中，各种势力盘根错节，像微缩版诸侯割据似的，相当难应付。
t_en: Ah... I don't know if I'd say that. Though the Alchemy Commission is small, there are complicated relations running between various different forces. It's like a miniature feudal state. It can be quite challenging to deal with.

t_ch: 妾身本想快刀斩乱麻，尽快清除掉几个拉帮结派的害虫。可他们偏偏占据着关键岗位，我手头又一时找不出更合用的人才，实在没办法直接把他们都清退。
t_en: Originally, I planned to make quick and decisive action and clear out these clique-forming pests as soon as possible. However, they have occupied key positions and I cannot currently find suitable personnel to take their places, so there is no way for me to simply get rid of them.

t_ch: 就喜欢这种权力相争的故事。
t_en: I love these kinds of stories of power struggles.

t_ch: 恕我直言，你喜欢的不是「温吞的权利相争」。而是「撕破脸的瞬间」、「得势之人的落差」、或者说是「戏剧化的冲突」？
t_en: If I may say so, I can't imagine that you enjoy this kind of "tepid stalemate." I imagine you're more excited by "the rise against the tyrant," "the fall of the mighty," or the "dramatic conflict?"

t_ch: 对吧？如果是这样的话，我也喜欢。
t_en: Is that correct? If it is, I am the same.

t_ch: 不喜欢这种尔虞我诈的故事。
t_en: I don't like these stories of deceit and subterfuge.

t_ch: 哎，要是可以的话，我也希望世界上少一些勾心斗角，那多累啊。
t_en: Ah, if I had my way, there would be far less power struggles in this world. They are exhausting.

t_ch: 但如果不可以的话，累就累点吧，至少要得到我满意的结果就成。
t_en: If that is not possible, I shall just have to face the exhaustion. As long as I'm satisfied with the result.

t_ch: 不过若论起弄权，丹鼎司这几个宵小之辈实在难上得来台面。妾身虽不能直接清理掉他们，但削弱他们的影响力倒还是轻而易举。
t_en: But, when it comes to a wielding power, these Alchemy Commission knaves have no sway when it comes to the public eye. Though I may not be able to directly remove them, weakening their influence is a piece of cake.

t_ch: 这些人看似团结，其实各有各的目的、各有各的野心，只要激化他们彼此间的不信任，就能化整为零，将一个小团体拆分成很多个更小的团体。
t_en: Though they appear united in solidarity, they all have their own motives and desires. All I need to do is rouse mistrust between them and I can reduce them to nothing, breaking their group into myriads of tiny units.

t_ch: 到那个时候，他们就彻底没能耐掀起什么风浪啦～
t_en: When that time comes, they simply won't be strong enough to make any waves~

t_ch: 当然了，这也不过是缓兵之计。等妾身找到足以替代他们的人才，接下来就到清算的时间啦～哼哼～
t_en: Of course, this is just stalling to gain time. Once I've managed to find suitable candidates to replace them, it will be time for their reckoning~ Hehe~

t_ch: 你有什么养生建议吗？
t_en: Do you have any self-care recommendations?

t_ch: 唔…保证作息稳定和膳食平衡，多做些体育锻炼？妾身能给出的建议也就是这些了，换句话说，你若身体无恙，这也是最合理的建议了。
t_en: Umm... Ensure a stable balance of work and rest, make sure to eat properly and train regularly? This is really all the advice I can offer you. In other words, if you are in good health, this is the most logical suggestion.

t_ch: 说来惭愧，虽然妾身还算是精通医道，但若与那些第一流的医士相比，还是稍逊一筹。
t_en: Though it pains me to say it, though I am proficient in medicine, I still have a way to go to catch up with the elite healers.

t_ch: 灵砂大人不会是在谦虚吧？
t_en: Are you being modest, Master Lingsha?

t_ch: 哎，谦虚又不会让我的工作减少，你放心，我并非是自谦。
t_en: Ah, modesty will not decrease my workload. Fear not, I am not putting myself down.

t_ch: 司鼎可并不需要一流医士。
t_en: The Cauldron Master does not need any master healer.

t_ch: 呵呵，看来{NICKNAME}颇有些心得呀，这点倒是没错。
t_en: Hehe, I see you have some familiarity with the position, {NICKNAME}. You're quite right.

t_ch: 咱们罗浮仙舟就龙女大人这样的神医，和她相比，妾身治病救人的本事仍属于常人理解范畴内。不过，也正是因为如此，龙女大人的专家号可不好排队。
t_en: With miracle healers on the Xianzhou Luofu like the great Dragon Lady, my healing skills by comparison fall into the realm of things that the average person could come to comprehend. However, it's precisely for this reason that the queues at the Dragon Lady's clinic are ridiculous.

t_ch: 医士们水平，因经验、天赋、专业领域而截然不同。不同医士们各有擅长，需要有人协调安排不同的医士处理他们擅长的领域……
t_en: Healers differ greatly in terms of ability, experience, natural talent and area of expertise. Each healer has their own specialties, so it is necessary for someone to coordinate and organize healers based on their strengths and areas of practice...

t_ch: 这就是联盟委派妾身做罗浮司鼎的原因。有人负责治疗病人，有人负责治疗丹鼎司，所以你明白了吗？妾身的主治范围，稍微有些不同呢。
t_en: That was why the Alliance made me the Luofu's Cauldron Master. Some have a duty to treat the illnesses of people, and some have a duty to cure the illnesses within the Alchemy Commission. So, do you see? The area of my healing expertise is slightly different.

t_ch: 灵砂大人，你好香啊。
t_en: Master Lingsha, you smell lovely.

t_ch: …应该是在说熏香的事吧？
t_en: ...You mean the smell of the incense?

t_ch: 妾身原本就有异于常人的好嗅觉，再加上拜师朱明的炎庭君，在闻香识香用香之事上又有些心得体会。看来，你需要一些小建议？
t_en: I have always had a sense of smell that far surpasses others'. In addition to my experience of using, smelling, and learning about fragrances during my time with the Zhuming's Ardens Regia, I would say I know my stuff. Do you need some suggestions?

t_ch: 怎么识别香的好坏？
t_en: How do you tell if an incense is good or bad?

t_ch: 香嘛，首先得打开盒子闻一闻。要是香喷喷的，估计是加了香精，不太好。高级的木香，都是沉敛的，有自然清雅的气息。
t_en: When it comes to incense, the first thing to do is open the box and take a whiff. If the smell comes rushing forth, it's probably had added fragrance and is a cheap product. High quality wooden incenses are subtle, with a natural and elegant aroma.

t_ch: 再就是摸一摸，要是木纤维太多，会有些粗糙，要是太硬，说明添加了太多粘合剂。
t_en: Another way to tell is to touch it. If there are too many wooden fibers, it can end up feeling rough. If it's too hard, that means that too much adhesive was added.

t_ch: 最直接的方法自然是烤一烤，但别让它被点着了。仔细闻一闻「没有完全点燃」的状态，没有烟气干扰，更容易判断香的品质。
t_en: The most direct method to test it would just be to roast it, but not allow it to burn. Take a good smell when it is not properly lit, and you can get a clearer judgment of the scent's quality without the smoke interfering.

t_ch: 如何选择合适的香？
t_en: How do you choose a suitable incense?

t_ch: 我比较推荐你根据自己的喜好选择…如果还是不太清楚的，也许可以考虑一下不同的用香情景？
t_en: I would recommend that you select according to your own preferences... If you're not too certain, you can think about the different situations where you might use it?

t_ch: 进行打坐、冥想之类的活动时，可以试试醇厚感比较强的香。比如我在批复各种报告和材料的时候，就喜欢这类的香。
t_en: If you're in meditation or deep reflection, you can try a stronger and more potent fragrance. For example, I like incense like this when I'm reviewing various reports and materials.

t_ch: 要是你在品茶、阅读、弹琴的话，可以试试清雅甘甜的香。我听说列车领航员姬子女士喜欢泡一杯咖啡看看书，这类香就比较适合。
t_en: If you're enjoying tea, reading, playing an instrument, or other similar activities, you might want something with a lighter, sweeter fragrance. I hear that the Express Navigator Himeko enjoys reading with a cup of coffee. I imagine this kind of incense would be more suitable for her.

t_ch: 怎么样，还算是不错的建议吧？
t_en: What do you think, some pretty good suggestions, right?

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，下次再聊吧～如果在罗浮遇到什么伤病，随时来找妾身就好～
t_en: Sure, I'll talk to you again next time~ If you get injured or sick during your time on the Luofu, come and find me any time~

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，下次再聊吧～如果在罗浮遇到什么伤病，随时来找妾身就好～
t_en: Sure, I'll talk to you again next time~ If you get injured or sick during your time on the Luofu, come and find me any time~

t_ch: 想了解一些事…
t_en: There are things I want to find out more about...

t_ch: 本座受惠于遍智天君，时间充足的话，满足他人的好奇也算是分内之事。
t_en: As someone who receives the Wisdomwalker's grace, satisfying the curiosities of the people is also part of my job. I'd love to do so if time allows.

t_ch: 将军体验卡，你会怀念吗？
t_en: Do you miss your General trial-period privileges?

t_ch: 怀念？哼，本座是要做将军的人，不需要怀念，未来有的是机会。
t_en: Do I miss them? Hmph, I will be the General one day, and there is no need for me to look back on them longingly.

t_ch: 不过，那些日子代行将军职务，确实有不一样的体会——
t_en: Although, my days acting as a General were indeed a unique experience...

t_ch: 看不完的参考情报，批不完的军务报告，还要同天舶司处理外事，同工造司讨论修缮…如此一看，出征巡猎反而更像是休假。
t_en: I have endless pieces of intelligence to read and military reports to review. Besides, I also have to deal with external affairs with the Sky-Faring Commission and discuss the reconstruction work with the Artisanship Commission... With so much going on, any outing, even if it were on official business, would feel like a vacation to me.

t_ch: 难怪青镞看我的眼神里充满了疑惑。想必是见惯了景元平日里悠哉的样子，瞧我左支右绌，想看我能坚持到几时吧。
t_en: No wonder Qingzu looks at me with so much confusion in her eyes. She must be used to seeing Jing Yuan taking it easy on a regular basis, and watching me struggle to keep up must've made her wonder how long I can hold on in this post.

t_ch: 哼，本座还尚未使出全力呢。
t_en: Hmph, I have not even given it my all yet.

t_ch: 怎么才能像你一样有学问？
t_en: How can I become as knowledgeable as you?

t_ch: 很好，很好学。
t_en: I'm pleased to see you have a thirst for knowledge.

t_ch: 「多见者博，多闻者智，拒谏者塞，专己者孤」，多读书、多思考、多观察世间万物，学养自然会积少成多。
t_en: "Seeing Is Learning, Reading Is Growing. Isolation Is Desolation, Self-Full Is Self-Dull." Read more, think more, and observe more, and you'll naturally become knowledgeable along the way.

t_ch: 如今不少仙舟民心高气傲，自以为短生种的学养注定比不上自己…无论长生短生，本座还不到三十岁的时候，就已经比大部分几百岁的老家伙有学问了。
t_en: Nowadays, many Xianzhou residents arrogantly think that the short-life folks can never catch up with them in terms of knowledge... But knowledge cares neither your age nor your lifespan. By the time I was just under thirty years old, I had already surpassed the knowledge of many centenarian old-timers.

t_ch: 有人痴长上千年，脑子里空空如也，岁数都长到永狩原的狍子身上去了。所以你不必听信年长者的鬼话，学养和寿命没什么必然联系。
t_en: Some folks have been around for thousands of years, but their minds are as empty as a fawn on the Ever-Hunt Plains. So don't blindly believe old-timers' nonsense. Old age does not equal great wisdom.

t_ch: 太卜司的事务忙得过来吗？
t_en: How are you coping with your job at the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 根本忙不过来！本座向来严于律己，对公务毫不懈怠。
t_en: I'm snowed under with work! I've always been self-disciplined and taken my duties seriously.

t_ch: 可就算有机巧辅助，外加将作息安排的效率规划至极限…一人身兼两大责任，实在是太难为自己了。
t_en: But even with the help of ingenia and being as efficient as possible with my schedule, wearing two big hats at once is pushing me to my limits...

t_ch: 那为何执着于当将军？
t_en: Why do you insist on being the general, then?

t_ch: 在外人看来，本座是权欲熏心，又或者酷爱征伐，才会如此渴求持节将兵吧…这样的流言我听过不少，倒也无所谓。
t_en: Outsiders might see me craving military authority, then surmise that I am power-hungry or fanatical about warfare... I've heard many such rumors and have paid them no mind.

t_ch: 不过真相却和他们想的差之千里。我自幼受学卜算，穷究命运变通。但论及「命运究竟是否可以改变」，这个终极之问，却无力回答。
t_en: However, the truth is far from their assumptions. I have studied divination since I was a child and extensively examined the fickle outcomes of fate, yet I remain powerless before the ultimate question of whether destiny can be altered.

t_ch: 本座曾为此与人有过一番辩诘。可惜如今，能为辩论画上句号的只有我一个了。
t_en: I once had a debate with someone over this issue. Unfortunately, I alone remain in the world of the living to bring the debate to a close.

t_ch: 那人告诉我，世间命途变化转折最为剧烈之处，便是战场。人们的胜负生死在此颠倒变化，莫甚于此。
t_en: That person told me the battlefield is where the most drastic changes of fate take place. Nowhere else do the destinies of human beings rise and fall with such volatility.

t_ch: 我要寻得卜者最想要的解答，来告慰那人的在天之灵。
t_en: I want to find this answer, this most sought-after of all answers for us diviners, to appease that person's spirit.

t_ch: 我该走了，给我算一卦吧。
t_en: Please do a divination for me before I go.

t_ch: 今日运势无咎，放心去做想做的事吧。
t_en: You have nothing to watch out for today. Be free to do whatever you desire.

t_ch: 想再问些「建木危机」的疑惑…
t_en: I'd like to know more about your suspicions about the "Ambrosial Arbor Crisis."

t_ch: 无妨，本座可以帮你简要梳理一下思路。
t_en: Go ahead. I might be of help in sorting out your thoughts.

t_ch: 那个「停云」一直是幻胧假扮的？
t_en: Has that "Tingyun" been Phantylia all along?

t_ch: 至少在你们登上仙舟之后见到的「停云」是幻胧假扮的。
t_en: I can only declare with certainty that the Tingyun you saw after you boarded the Xianzhou was impersonated by Phantylia.

t_ch: 至于再往前什么时候她被掉包，而真正的停云又在哪里，又或者说停云早已不在了…只能说信息太少，本座也未能知道更多。
t_en: As for when the real Tingyun was replaced, where she might be right now, and whether she had already passed away... I don't have any more information than you do.

t_ch: 但是天舶司接渡使停云是货真价实的，所有人有关她的回忆也更是如此。
t_en: But Tingyun the Sky-Faring Commission Amicassador did exist, and so does everyone's memory of her.

t_ch: 希望这样的解释能让你好受一些。
t_en: I hope this will make you feel better.

t_ch: 建木之乱后，联盟的态度如何？
t_en: What is the Alliance's attitude to all of this, now the Ambrosial Arbor crisis had ended?

t_ch: 联盟的态度…既然你见到了飞霄将军、怀炎将军，自然清楚联盟的态度。
t_en: About the Alliance's attitude... You have met with General Feixiao and General Huaiyan, so you are naturally aware of the Alliance's attitude...

t_ch: 联盟信任景元将军的处置，不过仍然有一些胆小怕事的家伙在摇动唇舌，不过这些已经不打紧了，联盟面临烬灭祸祖的威胁已经是不争事实了。
t_en: The Alliance has faith in General Jing Yuan's abilities to handle matters, but there are still a number of cowards that let their tongues wag freely. However, this is no longer important. The fact that the Alliance is faced with the descending threat of the Ruin Author is no longer a matter of truth or fiction.

t_ch: 我该走了，给我算一卦吧。
t_en: Please do a divination for me before I go.

t_ch: 今日运势无咎，放心去做想做的事吧。
t_en: You have nothing to watch out for today. Be free to do whatever you desire.

t_ch: 想再问些「太卜出差」的事情…
t_en: I'd like to know more about your "divination trip..."

t_ch: 哎，这罗浮仙舟要是没了我该怎么办？
t_en: Ah, what would the Xianzhou Luofu do without me?

t_ch: 你出差期间呼雷越狱了…
t_en: Hoolay escaped from prison while you were gone...

t_ch: 唉…早在本座出发前，就算到「罗浮」必有劫难。可与爻光将军所商议的要事，又是非本座亲临不可的。
t_en: Ah... I had calculated that the Luofu would face calamity long before I had departed. However, the important matters that I had to discuss with General Yao Guang demanded my presence in person.

t_ch: 不得已，本座只得将卜算结果呈给景元，然后惴惴不安地在「玉阙」开会。
t_en: My only choice was to report the results of my divination to Jing Yuan and impatiently await as I held the meeting at the Yuque.

t_ch: 好在，有景元的策略，又有另外两位天将的勇武与老练，这桩呼雷之乱总算是没有酿成大祸……
t_en: Fortunately, with Jing Yuan's strategy and the courage and experience of the other two Arbiter-Generals, we were able to prevent Hoolay's rebellion from becoming a complete catastrophe...

t_ch: 哦，当然了，还要感谢你，{NICKNAME}。也请替我把感谢带给丹恒和三月七。你们又帮了「罗浮」一个大忙，这人情债景元怕是还不完了，哈哈。
t_en: Oh, of course, I would like to thank you as well, {NICKNAME}. Please pass on my gratitude to Dan Heng and March 7th, also. You greatly assisted the Luofu once more. I fear that this is a debt that Jing Yuan will never be able to repay, haha.

t_ch: 有没有可以透露的消息…
t_en: Do you have any information you can share with me?

t_ch: 你是说那两位重犯？还是说玉阙仙舟的见闻？
t_en: You mean regarding the two felons? Or about the Xianzhou Yuque?

t_ch: 抱歉，眼下还不是随便打听的时候。不过，本座保证，等到可以分享情报的时候，我们肯定会告知星穹列车，毕竟你们是仙舟联盟的盟友。
t_en: My apologies. Now is not the time for such casual inquiries. However, please rest assured that when I am able to share my information, I will inform the Astral Express, as you are allies of the Xianzhou Alliance, after all.

t_ch: 我该走了，给我算一卦吧。
t_en: Please do a divination for me before I go.

t_ch: 今日运势无咎，放心去做想做的事吧。
t_en: You have nothing to watch out for today. Be free to do whatever you desire.

t_ch: 我爸是最好的飞行士！
t_en: My dad is the best pilot ever!

t_ch: 你吹牛！
t_en: You're bluffing!

t_ch: 我没有！
t_en: Am not!

t_ch: 那你会修星槎吗？
t_en: So can you repair starskiffs?

t_ch: 我学的不是这个！
t_en: That's not my area of education!

t_ch: 切，那有什么用。
t_en: Tsk, useless.

t_ch: 多吃一点，多吃一点！
t_en: Eat up. Eat up!

t_ch: 这味道…太可怕了！
t_en: This tastes... horrible!

t_ch: 好久没遇这种大事了…
t_en: It's been a long time since I ran into something this serious...

t_ch: 我一紧张就肚子疼…
t_en: My stomach hurts whenever I get nervous...

t_ch: 怎么又这样啊？
t_en: Again?

t_ch: 我也没办法啊…
t_en: I can't help it...

t_ch: 你这样怎么能入远征部队？
t_en: How'd you ever manage to get onto an expedition team then?

t_ch: 和丰饶孽物打？饶了我吧！
t_en: Fighting Abominations of Abundance? Spare me!

t_ch: 我专门赶过来看建木的。
t_en: I came specifically to see Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 真的又长起来了吗？
t_en: Is it really growing again?

t_ch: 当然，我还能骗你。
t_en: Yes. Would I lie to you?

t_ch: 我是不信世上有鬼的…
t_en: I don't believe in ghosts...

t_ch: 但打过几轮以后我信了。
t_en: But after playing a few hands with them, I've come to believe in them.

t_ch: 我不是很会打…
t_en: I can't really play...

t_ch: 你们让着我一点，谢谢。
t_en: Let me have this, please.

t_ch: 要打就快打。
t_en: If you wanna play, then play.

t_ch: 别催我呀，我要瞎打了。
t_en: Don't rush me or I'll be playing blind.

t_ch: 我不会打，各位高抬贵手。
t_en: I can't play. Please everybody, hands up.

t_ch: 不会打，我就教你嘛。
t_en: I'll teach you how if you don't know how to.

t_ch: 牌从门前过，不如摸一个。
t_en: If a tile's been passed, just draw another.

t_ch: 我就是客气客气而已。
t_en: I'm just being polite.

t_ch: 我和了。
t_en: I win.

t_ch: 快，打完这轮去避难！
t_en: Hurry up, finish up this game and evacuate!

t_ch: 都打了十轮了！
t_en: It's been ten games!

t_ch: 可是还没分胜负！
t_en: But we haven't got a winner yet!

t_ch: 继续！血战到底！
t_en: Keep going! Fight to the death!

t_ch: 你听说了吗？建木…
t_en: Have you heard? The Ambrosial Arbor...

t_ch: 别让我分心。
t_en: Enough distractions!

t_ch: 打牌重要还是建木重要？
t_en: What's more important — playing tile games, or The Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 废话，当然是打牌。
t_en: What nonsense, of course it's tile games.

t_ch: 这字我咋看不懂？
t_en: How come I don't recognize this character?

t_ch: 你懂什么，这叫草书！
t_en: What would you know? This is called cursive script!

t_ch: 这才叫真正的文学！
t_en: This is the stuff of true literature!

t_ch: 文学的未来属于网文…
t_en: The future of literature belongs online...

t_ch: 网文太长，动不动连载两百年…
t_en: Web articles are too long, and they've all got two hundred years of backstory...

t_ch: 未来的文学…
t_en: The future of literature...

t_ch: 必须局限在<unbreak>140</unbreak>字以内！
t_en: Must be confined to <unbreak>140</unbreak> characters or fewer!

t_ch: 那本小说可好看了！
t_en: That's a great book!

t_ch: 都是编的吧…
t_en: It's all made up...

t_ch: 谁也没见过十王司判官…
t_en: Nobody has ever seen any Judges of the Ten-Lords Commission...

t_ch: 辛苦你们协助巡防了…
t_en: Thanks for helping out on patrol...

t_ch: 都是为了仙舟…
t_en: We do it for Xianzhou...

t_ch: 嗯，都是为了仙舟！
t_en: Well, it's all for Xianzhou!

t_ch: 你看到了吧？那棵树…
t_en: Can you see it? That tree over there...

t_ch: 书里写过这种事情对吧？
t_en: This is covered in the book, right?

t_ch: 我不看书，别问我。
t_en: I don't read. Don't ask me.

t_ch: 这预示着大难临头啊！
t_en: This heralds great misfortune!

t_ch: 一定是寿瘟祸祖干的！
t_en: This must be the work of the Plagues Author!

t_ch: 都是业余乐手…
t_en: Amateurs...

t_ch: 大家喜欢就好…
t_en: I'm glad you like it...

t_ch: 洗油烟机～修理星槎～
t_en: Clean the cooker hood~ Repair the starskiff~

t_ch: 做工精良，绝世美玉！
t_en: Good workmanship. Exquisite jade!

t_ch: 老板，多少钱？
t_en: How much for this, boss?

t_ch: 八万一串，概不讲价。
t_en: Eighty thousand a piece. No discounts.

t_ch: 二十卖吗？
t_en: Can I have it for twenty?

t_ch: 诸位少在外面闲逛！
t_en: Everybody, please don't wander around outside!

t_ch: 早点回家，读书看报！
t_en: Let's go home soon and catch up on our reading!

t_ch: 或者听听小曲儿也好！
t_en: Or we can listen to some fresh new beats!

t_ch: 总之少在外面乱晃！
t_en: In short, stop lurking about outside!

t_ch: 这棵树都枯萎了几千年…
t_en: This tree has been withered for thousands of years...

t_ch: 居然重新长起来了…
t_en: But now it's actually growing again...

t_ch: 传说里的故事都是真的？
t_en: Are the rumors and legends about it all true?

t_ch: 可能吧，传说里是这样的…
t_en: Maybe. You know how the legend goes...

t_ch: 无尽形寿来自建木的果实。
t_en: An endless lifespan comes from the fruits of the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 那咱们也能长生不老啦？
t_en: Then can we live forever too?

t_ch: 只有你们化外民这样想。
t_en: Only Outworlders like yourself think like that.

t_ch: 我听说你家宝贝走丢了。
t_en: I heard your fur baby got lost?

t_ch: 我刚在地衡司登记完。
t_en: I just finished registering with the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 地衡司找猫找狗有一手。
t_en: They're good at catching stray pets.

t_ch: 他们就这点儿本事了。
t_en: And that's all they're good for.

t_ch: 可别小瞧本执事…
t_en: But I, an officer, am different...

t_ch: 我可是地衡司格斗第一！
t_en: I'm the best fighter in all of the Realm-Keeping Commission!

t_ch: 连续三年九极拳冠军！
t_en: I'm reigning martial arts champ, three years straight!

t_ch: 治安没出问题吧？
t_en: Any problems with public security?

t_ch: 暂时一切都井然有序…
t_en: Everything's in order for now...

t_ch: 那就好，那就好。
t_en: That's good, very good.

t_ch: 希望之后也别出乱子…
t_en: I hope there's no trouble in the future...

t_ch: 这个时间把我叫出来…
t_en: Why are you calling me this late...

t_ch: 难道说，你其实是想…
t_en: Don't tell me you're planning on...

t_ch: 没错！我有话要对你说。
t_en: That's right! I have something to tell you.

t_ch: 可是，我还没有准备好。
t_en: But I'm not quite ready.

t_ch: 你能帮我加班吗？
t_en: Can you work overtime for me, please?

t_ch: 我明天有事，拜托你了。
t_en: Please, I have something on tomorrow.

t_ch: 此情此景，我应该写首诗！
t_en: This moment right now... I ought to write a poem!

t_ch: 我满腹经纶，应该可以…
t_en: I'm a walking encyclopedia. It should be fine...

t_ch: 啊！花！你是多么地花！
t_en: O flowers! How flowery you are!

t_ch: 啊！草！你是多么地草！
t_en: O grass! How grassy you are!

t_ch: 啊！水！你是多么地水！
t_en: O water, how watery you are!

t_ch: 啊！我是多么地忧伤。
t_en: O, and how melancholic I am.

t_ch: 真是一首好诗！好诗！
t_en: What a beautiful verse! Well done~
